strengths: Strong distributed-systems and event-driven architecture experience (e.g., Dunnhumby, GISCollective)
Proven ability to build scalable microservices and real-time systems (e.g., Node.js, Dlang, Kubernetes)
Recent involvement in AI-agent infrastructure (re:cinq) and developer tooling (MCP)
concerns: Limited direct fintech or credit-risk experience in resume or current roles
No explicit mention of multi-agent AI systems in production or agentic frameworks beyond tooling
Lacks demonstrated leadership in scaling engineering teams to 30+ or governance of external delivery partners
response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo demonstrates strong backend and distributed-systems expertise with event-driven and real-time systems experience, and has recently engaged with AI-agent tooling. However, the resume lacks direct evidence of multi-agent AI systems in production, fintech-domain experience, or leadership scaling teams to senior levels. The candidate’s background is more aligned with general software engineering than the specific executive demands of a CTO in agentic AI-driven fintech infrastructure.
